agent_raw: The backtest for this fraud model is not sufficient to support the proposed auto-hold deployment. Three gaps are material. First, transactions blocked by existing rules were excluded from development. This is standard model-building practice, but it means the evaluation may not fully characterize model behavior on the live order queue if the model is not operating in a strict second-filter role after the rules engine. Second, the evaluation used fully matured labels from the most recent non-holiday period. Holiday season is the proposed deployment window, and holiday fraud patterns (gift card abuse, electronics account takeovers) may differ structurally from the evaluation period. Prior holiday seasons with now-mature labels should have been included as a held-out test. Third, the deployment is for automatic order holds during peak delivery season. False holds on legitimate orders at this time have asymmetric customer harm: missed delivery deadlines, forced cancellations, and potential churn of high-value customers. This harm is not captured by AUC or false positive rate. A shadow deployment phase and customer harm analysis are necessary before activating auto-holds.
all_issues_raised: Excluded pre-blocked transactions may misrepresent live queue
Mature-label period excludes holiday fraud patterns
Customer harm from false holds not quantified — AUC insufficient for deployment decision
